feed.title: SAP Community - PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance
feed.subtitle: PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Issue with : Perform Maintenance Jobs not working for goods return
entry.content: PMJ do not work for goods return - even all correct and qty issued is more then return we allways get that error message that 'You have entered a post quantity that is higher than the planned quantity' - looks like some bug in PMJ App as same Order can be posted return using Post Goods Movement

entry.title: Manage Maintenance Orders Cost Visibility
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am experiencing an issue with the SAP Fiori application Manage Maintenance Orders(F5241) regarding cost visibility. 

In ıw32 , planned and actual costs on a maintenance order are populated correctly based on the components (materials) entered in the order. 

However, when accessing the same maintenance order through the  'Manage Maintenance Orders' app, the costs section displays the following message: "There are no costs with the selected G/L account hierarchy.''

The application requires a **G/L Account Hierarchy** to be selected along with a ledger and currency, but no default hierarchy is being proposed, and the cost data does not populate. Additionally, currency and ledger fields appear to be inactive.

As far as I understand, the G/L Account Hierarchy configuration is missing; otherwise, could the issue be caused by something?  Is there a way to set a **default G/L account hierarchy** for the Fiori app? In settings can set it actually if its true.

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: SAP PM Custom Report Logic Validation for Equipment Running Hours
entry.content:
In our report requirement, we need to identify the correct Measuring Point for values such as Running Hours 

Currently, we are considering two possible approaches:

Option 1:
Use Measuring Point Description from IMPTT-PTTXT, for example:
PTTXT = 'RUNNING_HOURS' or PTTXT CP 'RUNNING_HOURS'

Option 2:
Use Characteristic Name from CABN-ATNAM, for example:
ATNAM = 'RUNNING_HOURS', derive ATINN, and then use characteristic-related logic to identify the relevant object/value.

Question:
Which approach is more technically reliable and functionally recommended for a custom SAP PM report?

Should we identify the Measuring Point using IMPTT-PTTXT, or should we use Characteristic Name CABN-ATNAM / ATINN as the main logic?

Also, is wildcard search on PTTXT, such as RUNNING_HOURS, advisable, or should we avoid it due to the risk of fetching multiple/wrong measuring points?

entry.title: BADI for Mass Schedule Maintenance Plans
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a requirement to trigger an Email Notification when Order gets created based on the System date(Order Call date) using Mass Schedule Maintenance Plans app.

Could you please provide if any BADI is available to implement this change in SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602?

entry.title: ITOB_CONSUMPTION_UPDATE BAPI INPUT PARAMETER
entry.content:
In ITOB_CONSUMPTION_UPDATE BAPI

For the input parameter I_STATION only three characters are allowed, generally in IFCU transaction we will enter Equipment and Gas station where it is 4 characters but in the BAPI for the field I_STATION it is allowing only three characters can anyone explain what value to update in the I_STATION input field and is there any standard relation available for gas station and service station

entry.title: Diesel consumption measurement document in SAP PM
entry.content:
I'm having trouble tracking diesel consumption for a specific plant. The transaction used for consumption is IFCU. The sequence is to create the equipment, create the metering points, and then a refueling order. When trying to record the deduction, the tab for entering the results remains closed. See image below: 

Here is an image of the equipment that provides the consumption figures. This one is from a different plant:

  

 Thank you very much in advance.

entry.title: Update settlement rule of maintenance order
entry.content:
Our requirement is to automatically default the settlement rule depending on the technical object type of the equipment or functional location. The receiver will be based on a custom table, with fields such as the technical object type, settlement category, receiver, and order type at least. The current configuration is that the settlement rules are generated during release. Since there are a couple of ways to release an order (via GUI tcodes, WDA apps, UI5 apps), the problem is which exit/badi/bapi to use. I have read that the exit EXIT_SAPLCOI1_027 is only applicable for GUI transactions and this is what our developer also mentioned. How else can we cater to the initial requirement where all possible entry points are covered?

Any input is greatly appreciated!

Regards,

Joanna

entry.title: Equipment User Status Functionality in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

The client has a requirement to maintain Equipment User Status in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. The status should be updated automatically based on Maintenance Request processing through additional business logic/custom logic.

However, we could not find standard functionality for Equipment User Status management in the Public Cloud system.

Current requirement examples:

  • If equipment is under breakdown maintenance request → automatically update status as “Breakdown”
  • If equipment is idle/running with zero KM/hours → automatically update status as “Idle”
  • Status should be reflected at Equipment master level automatically based on business conditions

We would like guidance on the following:

  1. Is Equipment User Status functionality available or planned for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  2. Is there any standard alternative recommended by SAP?
  3. Can this requirement be achieved using Custom Logic (BAdI), Custom Business Objects, Characteristics/Classifications, or side-by-side extensibility?
  4. Are there any released APIs/CDS Views/BAdIs available for updating custom equipment statuses automatically?

As a temporary workaround, we are considering using Classification Characteristics as a custom status field, but this has limitations for mass processing and standard status management.

Kindly advise the best recommended approach for Public Cloud.

Thanks & Regards

Mohit Mishra

@SAP @SAPPM @SAPEAMPS

entry.title: Display Notifications List(IW29) Fiori App- There is no Spreadsheet option to downloadd
entry.content:
Hi SAP, 

Issue is quite simple.

IW29 Display PM Notifications Fiori App. not showing the option- Spreadsheet when try to download, it's happening in our QA@ Quality system, but this is there in our Production(PA1) system. I have attached screengrabs which are self-explanatory.

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Question 9 is incorrectly phrased in SAP Certified - Implementation Consultant for SAP S/4HANA Exam.
entry.content: I am not able to get the plan date to be 10 days before the call date when I schedule my maintenance plan. The exam question is attached and above is my solution for it. Please someone assist me urgently to solve this out.

entry.title: how to use "teams" within plant maintenance work centers when mulitple work centers but same "Team"!
entry.content:
@Gaayathri 

Greetings - we have a challenge within the setup of responsibility management, and the assignment of teams to Plant Maintenance Work Centers. I would be interested to recieve any suggestions of how we could setup the structures to enable the following.

We have two product lines, A and B. There is a team of 3 technicians for Product Line A, and a team of 2 for Product Line B. We want the list of "jobs" within the app Perform Jobs to list those available for the team based on Product Line.

Now, here is the rest of our setup

Product LineLocationCost CenterPlant Maintenance Work CenterTeam linking to "Perform Jobs" APP
ABuilding 00110001000-001Same team for product line A
ABuilding 00220002000-002Same team for product line A
BBuilding 00330003000-003Team for Product Line B

We have different work centers so that time booked is booked against the correct cost center based on location.

Here are the constriants we have come across when defining the teams, and the issues they create.

ConstraintsIssue
ONLY 1 team per Single Work Center - cannot allocate multiple work centers to a single teamNeed separate teams for work centers 1000-001 and 2000-002 - although the "team" of technicians is the same group of people - so they should be able to see all jobs for Product Line A, no matter what location.
ONLY able to assign member to 1 team, not against multiple teamsOnce a member is assigned say to the team linked with Work Center 1000-001 they cannot also be assigned to the team linked to Work Center 2000-002
Each team requires a memberIssue - once assign one of the tech team to one team, cannot assign to a second - so even if we used the team hierarchy we HAVE to assign someone to the lower level team, so they cannot then be assigned at the higher level in order to see all Product Line A maintenance jobs.

We want the technicians for Product Line A to view all the "team" jobs for product line A, but this does not seem possible given the team constraints mentioned above.

Does any one have any ideas/ suggestions of how we could proceed?

Many thanks

Adrian

entry.title: Issues in Perform Maintenance Jobs
entry.content:
Hi Experts!

I have a few questions about this application. Questions 1 and 2 are particularly critical for me as they are affecting the process.  Thank you very much in advance.

1. When I try to record time confirmation using "SA" (Saat, which means Hour), the system gives the following error:

"Unit of measure H has not been created in language TR."

However, we do not use "H". We use the Turkish unit "SA" for hours. 

What is interesting is that the system allows me to save confirmations in Turkish when using dakika(DAK- which means minute). 

Also, if I  change the unit to another unit of measure and then switch back to "SA" (hours), the system allows the save successfully.

This behavior makes me think the issue may not actually be related to the missing of unit "H" in Turkish, because i can actually save with sa. Additionally, I do not receive this error when I select English as the system language or using F2173(Find Maintenance Order and Operation)-IW41

2. When I try to enter a measurement and click save, the system does not save it, and I also cannot see the measurement entry in IK18.  When I save  measurement entry in another project, it is saved successfully. In this system, I was not able to activate the WDA measurement applications. Could this be related?

3. When I try to return part of a planned material, the system adds a new line below, and this line is also added to the order.

When I enter the quantity to be returned, the system does not immediately process or update it, and it still appears as 0. 

However, after entering the return quantity in the order, the “Post” button for the return line becomes active in the application, allowing me to complete the return.

Is this the expected process flow, or is there an issue here?

4.  I also receive this warning when using unplanned materials;

Is there really no way to return the material in this scenario? There should still be some way to process a return, shouldn’t there?

entry.title: Configuration of WDA PM applications
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am experiencing issues while activating PM WDA applications. The application screens are not loading properly, or the fields appear as disabled. This is the case for most WDA applications:

Create Maintenance Notification;

The “Change Maintenance Notification” screen appears to be the same as the create screen, as if the application is not separated into create, change, and display versions.

Create Measuring Point;

Create Maintenance Plan;

I have activated the relevant services via SICF, after checking them from the Fiori library. I have also activated some business functions, but this did not resolve the issue.

The applications seem as if they have not been fully installed or properly set up. Since same issue occurs across all applications,  I suspect that the root cause is the same. Thank you very much in advance.

entry.title: Earliest Start Date & Latest Start Date in PM order operation not getting updated after Scheduling
entry.content:
Hi all Experts,

I'm currently having issue where the Earliest Start Date and Latest Start Date in PM Order are not updated after performed manual Scheduling. 

In addition, i tested perform manual Scheduling in Quality system, the Earliest Start Date and Latest Start Date are updated accordingly, as expected. 

Here the scheduling parameter config for reference

Can any experts advise any setting/config or how to solve this issue? Thanks in advance!

 

Regards,

Atikah R

entry.title: Reusing FLOC Structure Indicator for Second Plant in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Greetings to one and all,

I am learning SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

I understand how to handle Functional Location structures in On-Premise using classic T-codes. However, now that I am working in the Cloud.

I created a Structure Indicator for Plant-1. Now, I need to replicate the exact same hierarchical model for Plant-2 with copy reference of existed structure indicator.

As a beginner, I need your support with two questions

  1. What is the easiest cloud-native method to copy the existing structure tree from Plant-1 to Plant-2 while changing the descriptions within FIORI APP TILES?

Thank you

entry.title: F2173 “Add Time Confirmation” requires Personnel Number while IW41 does not – Standard behavior?
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am currently testing the fiori App F2173 – Maintenance Orders and Operations on an SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 on-premise system. I found a behavior difference between the Fiori app and the classic GUI transactions regarding time confirmations. When I create a time confirmation from GUI (IW41), works correctly even if no personnel number is specified. From Fiori App F2173, using "Time Confirmations" --> "Add Time Confirmation", the system behaves differently:

  • If I specify a Personnel Number → confirmation is posted successfully
  • If I leave Personnel Number empty → error IW803 is raised: 

Observations:

- The maintenance order does NOT use the phase model

My question is this: 

 

  • expected standard behavior of F2173?
  • a known restriction/design difference versus IW41?
  • or potentially a bug/configuration issue?

Any clarification, SAP Notes, or design documentation references would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Offsetting maintenance items in a strategy plan.
entry.content:
Hello Community, 

Apologies if this has already been answered here.

I have multiple items in a maintenance plan. one plan has a separate package and the rest (four) have same packages but different tasklists which have been created. I run a simulation and I noticed that the four items with the same package have same start dates on the WOs. 

for me, I want one item with a different start date from the rest. or if possible, can start dates change for these four items with same packages? 

The reason for all these items in the same plan is to allow for easy reference as the process is to inspect before the main work is executed. but i just want to have the inspection on a different WO issues from the same plan prior to the main work. Please assist. Thanks for your time.

entry.title: Calibration/EAM Checklist in S4HANA EAM Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi All, 

I would appreciate your guidance on whether the Calibration process can be configured in SAP S/4HANA EAM Public Cloud. If so, could you please advise on the available configuration options/Scope Items that need to be activated in SAP S/4HANA EAM Public Cloud.

Also please help me about Shutdown / Refurbishment process in SAP S/4HANA EAM Public Cloud.

Regards,

Ashu

entry.title: Hello SAP Professionals,I would appreciate your guidance regarding my SAP career path.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Professionals,

I would appreciate your guidance regarding my SAP career path.

I started my career as an SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) end user and later completed SAP PM functional training. After moving into IT consulting, I joined an organization expecting to work in SAP PM Functional Consultant, but was assigned to an SAP MM support role, where I gained around 3 years of experience.

Recently, I moved to another project and have again been assigned to an SAP PM project. However, the project is primarily support-oriented and has limited learning opportunities. As a result, I feel that my experience is spread across both MM and PM, but I have not had the opportunity to gain deep expertise in either module.

Interestingly, I have been able to clear more interviews for SAP PM roles than SAP MM roles, which has made me question which path I should focus on for long-term career growth.

My questions to experienced SAP professionals are:

  1. Should I focus on SAP MM or SAP PM for the next phase of my career?
  2. Which module offers better long-term growth and opportunities?
  3. Given my background in Maintenance Planning, SAP PM, and SAP MM support, which path would help me become functionally stronger?
  4. Would it be beneficial to position myself as a combined MM + PM consultant, or should I specialize in one module?

I feel many consultants get assigned to projects based on organizational needs rather than career goals, making it difficult to build deep expertise in a specific module.

I am looking for guidance from experienced professionals so that I can make an informed decision and focus my efforts in the right direction.

Thank you in advance for your advice and suggestions.

entry.title: Career Crossroads: SAP MM vs SAP PM – Looking for Expert Opinions
entry.content:
Hello SAP Professionals,

I would appreciate your guidance regarding my SAP career path.

I started my career as an SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) end user as Maintenance Planner for Oil and Gas Industry and later completed SAP PM functional training. After moving into IT consulting, I joined an organization expecting to work in SAP PM Functional Consultant, but was assigned to an SAP MM support role, where I gained around 3 years of experience.

Recently, I moved to another Project and have again been assigned to an SAP PM project. However, the project is primarily support-oriented and has limited learning opportunities. As a result, I feel that my experience is spread across both MM and PM, but I have not had the opportunity to gain deep expertise in either module.

Interestingly, I have been able to clear more interviews for SAP PM roles than SAP MM roles, which has made me question which path I should focus on for long-term career growth.

My questions to experienced SAP professionals are:

  1. Should I focus on SAP MM or SAP PM for the next phase of my career?
  2. Which module offers better long-term growth and opportunities?
  3. Given my background in Maintenance Planning, SAP PM, and SAP MM support, which path would help me become functionally stronger?
  4. Would it be beneficial to position myself as a combined MM + PM consultant, or should I specialize in one module?

I feel many consultants get assigned to projects based on organizational needs rather than career goals, making it difficult to build deep expertise in a specific module. I am looking for guidance from experienced professionals so that I can make an informed decision and focus my efforts in the right direction.

Thank you in advance for your advice and suggestions.
